data.title: Roots Hits the Road with Limited Edition “Pit Stops Summer ‘26” Collection. A Cross-Country Capsule Series Inspired by Canada’s Most Iconic Food Stops
data.description: Roots is embarking on a summer journey with the introduction of Pit Stops Summer ‘26, a special capsule collection honoring six popular roadside locations across Canada. Teaming up with Toronto-based artist and illustrator Moya Garrison-Msingwana (GANGBOX), the collection merges fashion, cuisine, and cultural narratives with a distinct Canadian perspective. This limited-edition lineup showcases unique collaborations and aims to showcase the essence of Canadian culture.

data.title: World Foundation Raises $52.5 Million to Expand Its World ID Infrastructure as AI Transforms the Internet
data.description: In George Town, Cayman Islands, World Foundation has raised an initial $52.5 million through a 1-year locked token sale to strategic investors. The first close was led by Pantera Capital, with participation from Bain Capital Crypto, Eightco Holdings (NASDAQ: ORBS), Selini Capital, Susquehanna Crypto, and other investors. This funding reflects investors’ dedication to World's ongoing development and relevance. All WLD tokens sold in this fundraising round are subject to a 12-month lock-in period.

data.title: Des Moines Federal Home Loan Bank reveals Q2 2026 financial figures, declares dividend.
data.description: In recent developments, it has been reported that the unemployment rate in the country has increased to 6.2%, marking a rise of 0.4 percentage points from the previous month. This change represents approximately 1.2 million individuals out of work. Additionally, the stock market experienced a significant drop, with the main index decreasing by 500 points to 10,000 points. Experts attribute these shifts to the ongoing economic uncertainties and global market trends. The government has announced plans to address these issues through various stimulus measures and job creation initiatives in the coming months.

data.title: More Than 12,000 Seek Compensation Directly Through SCE for Eaton Fire Recovery
data.description: In Rosemead, California, Southern California Edison revealed that over 12,000 individuals have applied for compensation via its Wildfire Recovery Compensation Program. Pedro J. Pizarro, the president and CEO of Edison International, SCE's parent company, emphasized the significance of the robust participation. He highlighted the program's aim to offer a fair and straightforward route for affected community members to seek compensation. The focus remains on assisting people in comprehending the process effectively.
ECB's Lane: We'll guide inflation back to target in the next year or so.
